showcase.title: AI Study Coach Prototype
showcase.summary: Revision support with prompts, checks and next-step suggestions.
showcase.description: A student-built prototype that turns module notes into short revision questions, confidence checks and suggested follow-up tasks.
showcase.author: Prototype by Priya M.
showcase.problem: Students wanted structured revision checks without receiving complete model answers.
showcase.approach: The prototype uses staged prompts to ask one question at a time, waits for the student's answer and then gives feedback on confidence and gaps.
showcase.outcome: Test users reported that the sequence made revision feel more active and helped them decide what to revisit next.
showcase.reflection: The strongest version kept the AI in a coaching role and asked students to make the final judgement on what to revise.

showcase.title: Lab Notes to Action Plan
showcase.summary: A practical assistant for turning messy experiment notes into next-step checklists.
showcase.description: A lightweight student workflow that helps organise observations, uncertainties and follow-up actions after practical sessions.
showcase.author: Prototype by Noah S.
showcase.problem: Lab notes often contained useful observations but students struggled to turn them into follow-up study tasks.
showcase.approach: The prototype asks students to separate observations, measurements, possible errors and questions before generating an action list.
showcase.outcome: The resulting checklist helped students prepare better questions for lab demonstrators and seminars.
showcase.reflection: Keeping uncertainty visible was more useful than asking AI to produce a polished conclusion too early.
